title: lantana camara subsp. portoricensis
intro: Lantana camara subsp. portoricensis, also known as Puerto Rican buttonweed, is a plant species within the verbena family that is native to the Caribbean and has been naturalized in other regions. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and other ailments. The health benefits of Lantana camara subsp. portoricensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and soothe respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, allowing for a range of applications in traditional medicine.
